title: echinolobium biarticulatum
intro: Echinolobium biarticulatum, also known as the Chittagong teak, is a species of tree native to South Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly due to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids, which possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Echinolobium biarticulatum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The herbal preparations of this plant can be found in various forms, including extracts, capsules, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
